Israel gave rewards to their idols in the offerings presented to them. It is common for people who are stingy in religion to be extravagant with their desires. Those are considered idolaters who love a reward in earthly possessions more than a reward in God’s favor and eternal life. They are full of the joy of harvest, and have no desire to mourn for sin. When we make the world and its things our idol and our portion, it is right for God to show us our foolishness and correct us. No one may expect to live in the Lord's land who will not be subject to the Lord’s laws or influenced by his love. When we enjoy opportunities for spiritual growth, we ought to consider what we will do if they are taken from us. While the pleasures of fellowship with God are beyond change, pleasant places bought with money or where people deposit money are likely to be destroyed. No famine is as terrible as a famine of the soul.

There was a time when the spiritual leaders of Israel were devoted to the Lord, but now they were like a trap set to ruin people. The people had become as corrupt as those of Gibeah; and their crimes should be punished in the same way. At first God found Israel pleasing to Him, like grapes to a traveler in the wilderness. He saw them with pleasure as the first ripe figs. This shows the delight God took in them; yet they pursued idolatry.

God departs from a people or person when he withdraws his goodness and mercy from them; and when the Lord has departed, what can humanity do? Even though good things seem to remain for now, the blessing is gone if God is gone. Even children will perish with their parents. Divine wrath dries up the root and withers the fruit of all comfort; and the scattered Jews daily warn us to be careful not to neglect or abuse the gospel. Yet every punishment is not a drying up of the root. It may be that God intends only to punish so that the sap may return to the root, so there will be more of qualities like humility, patience, faith, and self-denial. It is right for God to bring judgments on those who disregard his offered mercy.
